bugs.error_message: clinicSession.init() permanently blocks retry after transient DB/network error — user stuck in personal workspace for entire session
bugs.root_cause: _load() returned void on res.error (DB error), then returned normally. init() always set _initialized=true after _load() returned, regardless of success or failure. Token-refresh guard (if _initialized return) then permanently blocked all future init attempts for the session. User had no way to recover clinic context without signing out and back in.
bugs.fix: _load() now returns boolean: true = loaded or confirmed no-clinic, false = DB/network error. init() only sets _initialized=true when loaded=true. Transient errors leave _initialized=false, allowing the next onAuthStateChange (hourly token refresh) to retry automatically.

bugs.error_message: Wave C3: three known pricing bugs — (1) extract_impl slot3 hardcoded 4500, (2) costBoneGraft||800 conflicts with catalog default 1500, (3) ANNUAL_CHECKUP=300 hardcoded in 3 separate places instead of getClinicPrice('annualCheckup')*2
bugs.root_cause: (1) buildRestorativeResult used literal 4500 instead of c.costs.implant; (2) costGraphPanel used ||800 fallback, 2x less than catalog default 1500; (3) ANNUAL_CHECKUP top-level const evaluated once at parse time before denaiPrefs available, and duplicated in 3 files
bugs.fix: (1) normalize() adds costs.implant via priority chain, buildRestorativeResult uses c.costs.implant; (2) all costBoneGraft fallbacks use getClinicPrice('boneGraft'); (3) ANNUAL_CHECKUP removed from global scope, computed locally as getClinicPrice('annualCheckup')*2 in each function; (4) all costEngine.js/calcAI.js/clinicalEngine.js || fallbacks now use getClinicPrice(); (5) confirmNewPatient() initializes cost fields from getClinicPrice() at creation time

bugs.error_message: Cloud prefs hydration applies new tooth system / currency without re-rendering — UI shows stale labels until next user action
bugs.root_cause: _applyToDom() only applies darkMode. After cloud-wins LWW merge in hydrate(), tooth SVG labels and monetary display surfaces remain stale because render(S) and buildDentalSVG() are not called
bugs.fix: Added denaiRefreshAfterPrefsHydrate() function declaration in index.html (window-accessible bridge). Called from prefsSync.hydrate() after _applyToDom() in the cloud-wins branch only. Calls buildDentalSVG() + render(S) safely wrapped in try/catch

bugs.error_message: toothSystem preference accepts any string — corrupt localStorage value would persist without validation
bugs.root_cause: _loadLocal, hydrate, and save all checked typeof === 'string' for toothSystem. Any arbitrary string would be accepted and stored (e.g. 'invalid', ''). formatTooth() falls back safely but the corrupt value persists across sessions
bugs.fix: Changed all three toothSystem checks to explicit enum validation: === 'universal' || === 'fdi'. Invalid values are silently ignored and the in-memory default ('universal') is kept

bugs.error_message: Pricing inputs accept arbitrarily large values — parseFloat('9999999') passes isFinite check and saves to prefs
bugs.root_cause: sett-price-input had min=0 but no max. Number.isFinite() passes for any finite number including millions. No upper bound was enforced on either the HTML input or the JS change handler
bugs.fix: Added max='99999' attribute to pricing input HTML template. Added || parsed > 99999 guard to the change handler validation. Both layers: HTML constrains the spinner UI; JS rejects direct typed values

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block

bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Production audit: site2 Missing tooth condition does not normalize site2Structure/site2EndoStatus
bugs.root_cause: site2ConditionSelect change handler only set S.site2Condition; when condition=Missing tooth, site2Structure could remain Good. ClinicalEngine normalize() received inconsistent site2 state.
bugs.fix: Added guard in site2ConditionSelect handler: when condition=Missing tooth, force S.site2Structure=Poor and S.site2EndoStatus=No RCT needed, and sync the visible select controls.

bugs.error_message: Production audit: denaiApplyCloudMerge() leaves what-if slider positions and notes textarea stale after cross-device cloud merge
bugs.root_cause: After Object.assign(S, stillHere) and render(S), tempState still held old patient values (sliders read tempState), and initClinicalNotes() was not called.
bugs.fix: Added tempState = {...S}, updateSliderPositions(), initClinicalNotes() after render(S) in denaiApplyCloudMerge().

bugs.error_message: Production audit: _checkEmitAnalysisCompleted() uses calcAI(S) — emits wrong rec metadata for restorative cases
bugs.root_cause: calcAI() returns implant/bridge scores for ALL condition types including restorative. For restorative cases the correct engine is ClinicalEngine.process(S). Workflow observability events had incorrect rec field.
bugs.fix: Replaced calcAI(S) with ClinicalEngine.process(S) in _checkEmitAnalysisCompleted(). ClinicalEngine routes to calcAI internally for missing-tooth cases so no behavioral regression.

bugs.error_message: Production audit: emergency save in beforeunload unreliable on iOS Safari — data in 200ms debounce window can be lost
bugs.root_cause: iOS Safari does not fire beforeunload reliably. Emergency save only ran in beforeunload. pagehide (which iOS fires reliably) only called CleanupRegistry.cleanup().
bugs.fix: Expanded pagehide handler to include full emergency save sequence. Natural idempotent guard: on desktop beforeunload fires first and clears _saveStateTimer, so pagehide skips the write.

bugs.error_message: Production audit: bone graft 0-dollar pricing broken — || operator treats 0 as falsy, falls through to clinic default
bugs.root_cause: All six costX fields used || operator. A clinic-configured price of 0 for any treatment (e.g. bone graft) would be silently ignored in favor of catalog default.
bugs.fix: Replaced || with != null ternary pattern in both costEngine.js and calcAI.js. Allows zero as a valid clinic price.

bugs.error_message: Production audit: importAllData() merges backup patients without StateValidator validation — corrupt values silently persist
bugs.root_cause: Object.assign({}, DEFAULT_STATE, p) fills missing fields safely, but out-of-range or wrong-type values from backup were accepted without checking.
bugs.fix: Added StateValidator.validateAll(imported) after merge. Invalid patients increment warnCount. Warning toast fires after import if warnCount > 0. Import not blocked.

bugs.root_cause: 5 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 18→23 lines (5 removed)

bugs.error_message: Extraction path (extract_impl) hidden for COMPROMISED + RCT done + posterior teeth — endocrown ternary blocked escalation from appearing
bugs.root_cause: generateTreatments slot3 ternary checked endocrownViable first, so when endocrownViable=true AND escalationViable=true the endocrown always won slot3 and extract_impl was never generated — invisible to clinician and recommendation engine alike
bugs.fix: Moved endocrown to slot1 (it is the most conservative full-coverage option for RCT-done posterior). Slot3 now always shows extract_impl when escalationViable=true. Added HOPELESS guard to suppress endocrown in slot1 for hopeless cases.

bugs.error_message: Poor hygiene had no numerical effect on crown prognosis score — only added a rationale string
bugs.root_cause: The calcAI.js crown scoring section included hygiene switches for implant and bridge (main section) but had no hygiene switch inside the crown scoring block. Poor hygiene only triggered a text rationale in scoreRestorative without reducing the crown score.
bugs.fix: Added hygiene switch inside the inner crownViable block in calcAI.js: Poor hygiene: crown -=3.5, conf -=3; Fair hygiene: crown -=1.0. Penalty propagates automatically to scoreRestorative via baseAI.crown.

bugs.error_message: Splinting overtreatment: all COMPROMISED cases triggered splinted crowns, including bruxism-only cases with intact abutments
bugs.root_cause: splintedPreferred included caseClass.type === CT.RESTORATIVE_COMPROMISED as a third condition. Since fairStructure+bruxism classifies as COMPROMISED, single posterior teeth with bruxism and healthy neighbors received splinting with no structural indication.
bugs.fix: Removed CT.RESTORATIVE_COMPROMISED from splintedPreferred. Now only poorFerrule or abutmentCompromised trigger splinting — the two evidence-based structural indications.

bugs.error_message: Bruxism has zero confidence effect in calcAI main section — missing-tooth cases with severe parafunction get identical confidence to no-parafunction profile
bugs.root_cause: The parafunction switch only existed inside the inner if(crownViable && crown > 0) block. calcAI main section (implant/bridge scoring) had no parafunction term at all. H1 deferred from Wave A.
bugs.fix: Added parafunction switch after occlusion switch in calcAI main section: Clenching conf−=2, Bruxism conf−=4 + zirconia reason, Both conf−=6 + reason. Confidence-only, no score change — preserves non-implant-phobia.

bugs.error_message: calcAIMulti confidence has no parafunction term — multi-tooth bruxism cases fully inert in confidence output
bugs.root_cause: The calcAIMulti confidence block only penalized poor bone, poor hygiene, and compromised abutments. Parafunction/bruxism was never read for the confidence calculation in the multi-tooth path.
bugs.fix: Added parafunction block after youngGoodBone check: Bruxism/Both → conf−=4 + reason, Clenching → conf−=2.

bugs.error_message: explain() factors in ClinicalEngine missing smoking, uncontrolledDM, and clenching-only signals — these risks present in calcAI but not surfaced in restorative explainability output
bugs.root_cause: explain() destructured only b, o, p, r from c — systemic (sys) was not destructured. No sys.currentSmoker, sys.uncontrolledDM, or o.clenching checks in the factors array.
bugs.fix: Added systemic:sys to explain() destructuring. Added three factor pushes: currentSmoker→neg, uncontrolledDM→neg, clenching&&!bruxism→warn.

bugs.root_cause: Had 'Active decay compromises bond quality — full cove
bugs.fix: Changed to 'Poor hygiene: resin bond degrades under bacterial

bugs.root_cause: Was returning: { rec, ideal, conf, confLevel: conf >= 80 ? 'High'
bugs.fix: Now returns: { rec, ideal, conf, confLevel: conf >= 75 ? 'High'

bugs.root_cause: 5 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 17→26 lines (5 removed)

bugs.error_message: Onlay ties crown at poor hygiene under maximal favorable conditions — sort order gives onlay slot1 priority (clinically wrong)
bugs.root_cause: At goodFerrule+3, !highLoad+1.5, isPosterior+1, !needsRCT+2, goodBone+0.5 (+8 total), onlay = 91+8-5 = 94. Crown at same conditions = 94. Tie. Stable sort gives onlay priority.
bugs.fix: Increased onlay poorHygiene penalty from -5.0 to -6.0. Now onlay = 93 < crown = 94 at maximal conditions with poor hygiene.

bugs.error_message: Preservation-bias recommendation does not reflect clinical ambiguity when extraction scored higher — confidence unchanged despite close call
bugs.root_cause: recommend() left conf unchanged when preservation bias fired with extraction scoring higher. A close preserve-vs-extract call should feel less certain but confidence was unaffected.
bugs.fix: Added preservation-tension softening: when biasFires && extractOpt.score > bestPreserve.score, conf reduced by 2/4/7 based on pressureGap (0-0.5/0.5-2/2+). COMPROMISED path only.

bugs.root_cause: 3 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 7→12 lines (3 removed)

bugs.error_message: splintedPreferred fires for anterior poor-ferrule teeth — 'Splinted Crowns' appears as slot2 for maxillary anterior teeth with poor ferrule only (no abutment compromise)
bugs.root_cause: splintedPreferred = b.poorFerrule || b.abutmentCompromised had no position guard. A maxillary anterior tooth with poor ferrule (rs=Poor) but no abutment compromise triggered 'Splinted Crowns' as slot2. Anterior splinting for ferrule loss alone is a specialist eye-roll moment — not clinically indicated without compromised abutments.
bugs.fix: Changed to (b.poorFerrule && t.isPosterior) || b.abutmentCompromised. Posterior poorFerrule still triggers splinting (load-sharing indication). abutmentCompromised still fires universally. Anterior poorFerrule alone → crown in slot2 instead.

bugs.error_message: Young patient (<40) extraction recommendation in COMPROMISED context carries same confidence as older patient — High confidence for 22yo anterior extraction is clinically alarming
bugs.root_cause: process() passed recResult from recommend() directly without any patient-age awareness. For a 22yo with a poor-ferrule anterior tooth where extraction wins in COMPROMISED context, conf was calculated from implant-viability factors only (good bone → High conf). No clinical signal that young patients should trigger more preservation hesitation.
bugs.fix: Added young-patient extraction restraint in process(): when caseClass.type === CT.RESTORATIVE_COMPROMISED AND recResult.rec === 'crown' (extraction wins) AND c.patient.young (age < 40), apply conf −6 and recalculate confLevel. Not applied in HOPELESS (extraction mandatory regardless of age). recResult changed from const to let to allow reassignment.

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Backup export missing clinic preferences (currency, toothSystem, pricing) — restore from backup lost clinic configuration
bugs.root_cause: exportAllData() preferences object only included darkMode. currency, toothSystem, and pricing from denaiPrefs_v1 were never serialized into the backup file.
bugs.fix: Extended preferences block in exportAllData() to include currency/toothSystem/pricing via denaiPrefs.get() with safe fallbacks. Extended _applyImport() to restore each field individually with per-field validation against allowlists. Old backups without these fields import silently.

bugs.error_message: Deleting the active patient leaves stale UI — ghost patient shown, subsequent saveState() re-adds deleted patient
bugs.root_cause: confirmDeletePatient() called closeDeletePatient() before checking if deleted patient was active. closeDeletePatient() sets _pendingDeleteId = null, so the check _pendingDeleteId === S.id at line 5488 evaluated null === S.id (always false for valid IDs). Result: when active patient deleted, switchPatient(list[0].id) was never called — S still pointed to deleted patient and renderPatientList() was the only action.
bugs.fix: Snapshot _pendingDeleteId to const _deletedId before closeDeletePatient() call. Check _deletedId === S.id instead of _pendingDeleteId. One-liner const capture at line 5483.

bugs.error_message: Phase 1.2: Legacy dandy* storage keys not migrated to denai* namespace
bugs.file: src/constants/storageKeys.js, index.html, src/sync/cloudSync.js, src/sync/prefsSync.js, tests/auth/runner.js, tests/sync/runner.js, tests/e2e/runner.js, privacy.html, terms.html
bugs.root_cause: STORAGE_KEY='dandyCaseState_v8', PATIENTS_KEY='dandyPatients_v2', dandyDarkMode were dandy-branded persistence keys from the pre-rename era. After renaming constants to denai* in storageKeys.js, existing users would lose all data because their data lived under old keys.
bugs.fix: 1) Updated storageKeys.js constants to denaiCaseState_v8 / denaiPatients_v2. 2) Added idempotent migration IIFE in index.html BEFORE const S = loadState() that copies old→new for all 3 keys if new key absent. 3) Updated dark mode read to check denaiDarkMode first, fall back to dandyDarkMode. 4) Updated dark mode writes (toggleDarkMode, denaiSetDarkMode) to write denaiDarkMode. 5) Updated cloudSync.js private PATIENTS_KEY. 6) Updated prefsSync.js init() reconcile to check denaiDarkMode first, fall back to LEGACY_DARK_KEY. 7) Updated flash prevention in index.html, privacy.html, terms.html to OR denaiDarkMode||dandyDarkMode. 8) Updated all 3 test runners. Old keys not deleted (non-destructive migration).

bugs.error_message: PHI cross-user contamination: pending sync queue ops flushed under a different user's identity after sign-out on shared device
bugs.root_cause: Sync queue is persisted in localStorage under QUEUE_KEY and is user-unaware. On signOut(), the queue was NOT cleared. If a different user signs in on the same device, flush() reads the old queue but uses the new user's session.user.id as user_id in the upsert row. patients_insert_own RLS evaluates user_id = auth.uid() — since user_id IS the new uid, the policy PASSES. Patient data from the previous user is uploaded to the new user's cloud account.
bugs.fix: Added abandonQueue() to syncQueue (clears _flushTimer + _queue + QUEUE_KEY from localStorage). Called from authModule.signOut() and from _listenAuthChanges else-branch (SIGNED_OUT). Local patient data in localStorage is untouched — cloudSync Pass 2 re-enqueues it on next sign-in.

bugs.error_message: Double hydrate/flush on every page load — INITIAL_SESSION event fires in addition to _restoreSession() scheduling
bugs.root_cause: Supabase onAuthStateChange fires INITIAL_SESSION synchronously at listener registration when a session exists. _restoreSession() already scheduled hydrate/flush via setTimeout(0). _listenAuthChanges() then also scheduled hydrate/flush for the INITIAL_SESSION event. Both setTimeouts ran, doubling page-load network traffic. _syncing/_flushing guards prevented data corruption but allowed redundant network calls.
bugs.fix: Added event === 'INITIAL_SESSION' early-return guard inside _listenAuthChanges after _setStatus. INITIAL_SESSION is skipped; all subsequent events (SIGNED_IN, TOKEN_REFRESHED, SIGNED_OUT) continue to trigger hydrate/flush as before.

bugs.error_message: Online reconnect triggers flush (write path) but NOT hydrate (read path) — changes from other devices not pulled for up to 1 hour after reconnect
bugs.root_cause: syncQueue.init() registers: window.addEventListener('online', flush). Only the write path runs on reconnect. If another device edited patients while this device was offline, those changes are invisible until the next token refresh (hourly onAuthStateChange SIGNED_IN event triggers hydrate).
bugs.fix: Added denaiCloudSync.hydrate() call alongside flush() in the online event listener. Both read and write paths now run on network reconnect.

bugs.root_cause: Had 'dandyActivePatient_v1'
bugs.fix: Changed to 'dandyCaseHistory_v1_'

bugs.error_message: schema.sql trigger creation not idempotent — re-applying to existing database fails at CREATE TRIGGER with duplicate_object error, blocking environment reconstruction
bugs.root_cause: CREATE TRIGGER profiles_updated_at and patients_updated_at were bare statements with no idempotency guard. Every other migration construct in the file uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS, or DO $$ EXCEPTION duplicate_object $$ — triggers were the only exception.
bugs.fix: Wrapped both CREATE TRIGGER statements in DO $$ BEGIN ... EXCEPTION WHEN duplicate_object THEN NULL; END $$ blocks. Consistent with the existing FK and policy idempotency pattern.

bugs.error_message: Missing composite index on clinic_members(user_id, clinic_id) — patients_select_clinic_member RLS policy EXISTS subquery runs a filter pass over user_idx rows instead of a single index-only lookup
bugs.root_cause: clinic_members_user_idx covered (user_id) only. The EXISTS check in patients_select_clinic_member requires both (clinic_members.user_id = auth.uid() AND clinic_members.clinic_id = patients.clinic_id). Without the composite index, PostgreSQL scans the user's membership rows from user_idx and filters on clinic_id — acceptable at 1-2 memberships but degrades as clinic_members grows.
bugs.fix: Added CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS clinic_members_user_clinic_idx ON clinic_members (user_id, clinic_id). Single index-only scan satisfies the EXISTS predicate entirely.

bugs.error_message: Dead variable ACTIVE_KEY declared but never used in cloudSync.js module scope
bugs.root_cause: var ACTIVE_KEY = 'dandyActivePatient_v1' was a legacy holdover from an earlier cloudSync design that used active-patient restoration. The active patient tracking was moved to the inline script (denaiApplyCloudMerge bridge). ACTIVE_KEY remained but was never referenced inside the module.
bugs.fix: Removed the dead variable declaration.

bugs.error_message: smoke-implant, smoke-bridge, smoke-restorative-visible fail: #crownRiskSection exists / #implantRiskSection exists checks fail after Phase 21 React extraction
bugs.root_cause: RiskPanelComponent returned null for inactive sections (crownSection = null when showCrown=false, implantSection = null when showImplant=false). Smoke tests use querySelector(#id) !== null — null React output removes the element entirely. Pre-React template always emitted both IDs, hiding inactive ones with display:none.
bugs.fix: Always render both #implantRiskSection and #crownRiskSection divs. Visibility controlled by style={{ display: none }} on the container when showImplant/showCrown is false. Content (RiskRow children) is only populated when risks data is present — but the container is always in the DOM.

bugs.root_cause: Had 'none'
bugs.fix: Changed to 'block'

bugs.error_message: smoke-restorative-visible: #implantRiskSection hidden — crownRiskSection not visible despite showCrown=true
bugs.root_cause: Two distinct bugs: (1) crownSection used style:undefined when showCrown=true, leaving CSS #crownRiskSection{display:none} to hide it despite React rendering showCrown=true; (2) implantSection used style:undefined (not style:{}) when showImplant=true, causing a property-addition transition (undefined->object) when switching to showImplant=false. React 18 concurrent mode can defer property-addition diffs differently from value-change diffs, leaving #implantRiskSection visible (no inline display:none applied) despite correct component props.
bugs.fix: Changed both sections to always use explicit {display:block|none} style objects. implantSection: {display:risks.showImplant?block:none}. crownSection: {display:risks.showCrown?block:none}. Always-object style eliminates undefined->object React diff edge case; React now does a value-to-value transition (block->none or none->block) which is applied reliably.

bugs.root_cause: Had 'none'
bugs.fix: Changed to 'block'

bugs.error_message: smoke-restorative-visible: #implantRiskSection hidden — React 18 async update rendering prevents smoke test assertions from seeing updated DOM
bugs.root_cause: React 18 createRoot().render() is synchronous ONLY for the initial mount. For subsequent update renders (scenarios 2 and 3 in the smoke loop), React schedules work via the Scheduler (MessageChannel) as an async macrotask. The smoke test runs synchronous assertions immediately after render() returns, before React flushes the update. The DOM retains scenario 1 state (showImplant=true for missing tooth) for all subsequent renders. This is why all prior style/logic fixes had no observable effect — the business logic (showImplant=false for RESTORATIVE_VIABLE) was correct, but the DOM was never being updated before assertions ran.
bugs.fix: Wrapped _riskRoot.render() with ReactDOM.flushSync() in reactBridge.js. flushSync forces React to synchronously commit all pending work before returning, restoring parity with the legacy synchronous imperative DOM mutations the smoke tests were designed against.

bugs.error_message: Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected end of input around: (index):4382 — raw JS visible on screen including generateReport(), openReport(), reportHistory logic
bugs.root_cause: Service worker registered with CACHE = 'denai-v2.0' caches SHELL = ['./'] (the main HTML) on install. The activate handler only deletes caches with names !== 'denai-v2.0', so a truncated/corrupted version of index.html cached in 'denai-v2.0' during R4.1 development was served indefinitely. The cached file was truncated at ~line 4382, causing V8 to throw 'Unexpected end of input' and rendering post-truncation script content as visible HTML text nodes.
bugs.fix: Bumped CACHE from 'denai-v2.0' to 'denai-v2.1' in the swCode template literal at index.html line 5945. This forces: (1) new SW install with fresh 'denai-v2.1' cache, (2) addAll(['./']) fetches the current complete file from network, (3) activate handler deletes stale 'denai-v2.0' cache permanently.

bugs.error_message: Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected end of input at line 4382; raw JS visible on screen; navigateTo is not defined — app fails to boot
bugs.root_cause: generateLabDocument() wrote </body></html> literally inside a template literal in an inline <script> block. Chrome's HTML parser exits script data state early on </body></html> (a real-world browser quirk, not a spec violation). This truncates the script block mid-template-literal, causing 'Unexpected end of input' and rendering the remainder as visible text. Node.js new Function() check passes because it bypasses the HTML parser. reportTemplates.js:60 already uses the ${'</'+'body></'+'html>'} split trick for exactly this reason — generateLabDocument() omitted it.
bugs.fix: Replaced literal </body></html>` at the end of generateLabDocument()'s html template literal with ${'</'+'body></'+'html>'}` — same split trick used in reportTemplates.js:60. Commit 7577ab7.

bugs.error_message: P0 clinical safety gap: calcAIMulti() had no age<18 implant deferral — teenager with two adjacent missing teeth received normal adult implant recommendation
bugs.root_cause: calcAI() (single-tooth path) applies implant -= 15.0; conf -= 15 for age < 18 at line 354. calcAIMulti() (multi-tooth path) checked age > 65 but had no lower-age guard. A patient age < 18 also triggered youngGoodBone bonus (+4.0 to implant2) with no offsetting skeletal-maturity penalty — actively encouraging implants in skeletally immature patients.
bugs.fix: Added age < 18 block to calcAIMulti() between the age > 65 check and the CLAMP section: implant2 -= 15.0; cantilever -= 15.0; conf -= 15. bridge4 deliberately NOT penalized (conventional bridge has no skeletal growth contraindication and is the standard adolescent gap treatment). Reasons and factors propagation mirrors calcAI() exactly.

bugs.error_message: P0 workflow gap: sendToLab() executed immediately on click with no confirmation — misclick locks treatment plan, emits wfHistory event, saves state, and generates lab document irreversibly
bugs.root_cause: sendToLab() had no guard before state mutation. First line was S.labStatus = 'pending' — any click triggered the full workflow transition including saveState(), _emitWfEvent('lab_sent'), renderWorkflowBar(), and generateLabDocument(). All other destructive transitions in Denai (deletePatient, deleteReportHistory) had confirmation guards; sendToLab() was the only unguarded consequential transition.
bugs.fix: Added if (!confirm('Send this case to lab? This will lock the treatment plan.')) return; as the first line of sendToLab(), before any state mutation. Cancel path is a pure no-op. Confirm path behavior is identical to pre-fix.

bugs.error_message: P0 disclosure-timing gap: bridgeWarning visible only after clinician selects bridge tx, not during recommendation evaluation — clinician could commit to bridge without seeing abutment-compromise context
bugs.root_cause: bridgeWarning condition was: stateObj.bone === 'Poor' && stateObj.tx === 'bridge'. Default state.tx is 'implant'. So for a poor-bone missing-tooth patient, the warning never entered the reasons array until the clinician clicked the bridge treatment card. The warning is clinical decision context — it should be visible during option evaluation, not only after selection.
bugs.fix: Removed stateObj.tx === 'bridge' from the condition. Warning now fires whenever stateObj.bone === 'Poor' in calcAI(). Surfaces through reasons array at analysis time for all MISSING_SINGLE cases. RESTORATIVE cases are unaffected: buildRestorativeResult() uses expl.reasons from explain(), not baseAI.reasons, so the warning never reaches the UI for restorative conditions (correct — bridge-as-abutment semantics don't apply to restorative mode).

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Lab sheet regenerated from live state — reprints after treatment/material edits show wrong fabrication spec
bugs.root_cause: generateLabDocument() read S.tx, S.selectedMaterial, S.occlusion, S.labNotes, and all AI clinical inputs (via _getAiForPlan(S)) from live mutable state. Any post-send edit to treatment selection, material, or clinical inputs changed the reprinted lab sheet.
bugs.fix: sendToLab() now freezes S.labSnapshot = { txLabel, txSummary, matLabel, matNote, labNotes, name, tooth, caseNum, sentAt } before state mutation. generateLabDocument() checks S.labSnapshot first — if present, uses frozen values; if null (pre-snapshot patients), falls back to live S. labSnapshot added to DEFAULT_STATE (null) and serializer ALLOWED_FIELDS (syncs across devices).

bugs.root_cause: Had 'In Analysis'
bugs.fix: Changed to 'complete'

bugs.error_message: caseStatusPill shows 'In Analysis' for delivered cases; markDelivered/reopenDelivery stale plan view; cAtLab double-counts labStatus='received' cases
bugs.root_cause: Three delivery-workflow inconsistencies: (1) stageLabel ternary had no case for 'complete' (returned by _getWorkflowStage when caseDelivered=true), falling through to 'In Analysis' default. (2) markDelivered() and reopenDelivery() refreshed lab+reports views but not plan view — plan banner showed stale delivery state. (3) cAtLab filter used p.labStatus (truthy) instead of p.labStatus==='pending', causing labStatus='received' cases to appear in both 'At Lab' and 'Delivered' pipeline counts simultaneously.
bugs.fix: 5 surgical edits: (1) Added wfStage==='complete' ? 'Delivered' case to stageLabel ternary at line 3727. (2) Added if(UIState.view==='plan') renderPlanView() to markDelivered() at line 2685. (3) Added if(UIState.view==='plan') renderPlanView() to reopenDelivery() at line 2694. (4) Changed cAtLab filter to p.labStatus==='pending' at line 2969. (5) Changed stageFilter.lab to p.labStatus==='pending' at line 2864.

bugs.root_cause: 2 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 28→57 lines (2 removed)

bugs.error_message: Cloud overwrite toast is generic ('Patient updated from another device') with no clinical context; non-active patient overwrites produce no notification
bugs.root_cause: denaiApplyCloudMerge() always showed the same generic toast regardless of what changed (delivery state, lab status, plan approval, treatment). Non-active patient changes were silently applied (list re-rendered only). No clinical context available to clinician deciding whether to investigate.
bugs.fix: Enhanced denaiApplyCloudMerge() with: (1) context-aware toast comparing _prevDelivered/_prevLabStatus/_prevPlanApproved/_prevTx to post-merge S values — highest-priority clinical change drives the message. (2) Brief count toast for non-active patient syncs when active patient is not also changing (avoids double-toast).

bugs.error_message: Endocrown pricing is hardcoded as crown×0.90 in clinicalEngine.js and absent from TREATMENT_PRICING_CATALOG — not configurable by clinics, not synced across devices
bugs.root_cause: restorativeCosts.slot1/slot3 used inline ratio `c.costs.crown * 0.9` for endocrown, bypassing the patient-override → clinic-preference → catalog-default priority chain. No costEndocrown field in DEFAULT_STATE or ALLOWED_FIELDS.
bugs.fix: B3: Promoted endocrown from derived to standalone configurable catalog entry (default: 1080 = crown×0.90). Added costEndocrown to TREATMENT_PRICING_CATALOG, DEFAULT_STATE, normalize() costs object, serializer ALLOWED_FIELDS, and new patient init. restorativeCosts.slot1/slot3 now reference c.costs.endocrown.

bugs.root_cause: 3 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 5→7 lines (3 removed)

bugs.error_message: Post-send edits to Lab Notes textarea are silently ignored on reprints; notes typed on Device A invisible when Device B sends to lab
bugs.root_cause: (1) labNotes not in serializer ALLOWED_FIELDS — device-local only, pre-send notes lost cross-device. (2) Textarea remained editable after sendToLab() but generateLabDocument() uses snap.labNotes (frozen at send time), not live S.labNotes — silent mismatch with no UI indication. (3) Section titled 'Manufacturing Notes' in lab view but 'Clinician Notes' in lab sheet — inconsistent vocabulary.
bugs.fix: B4: (1) Added 'labNotes' to ALLOWED_FIELDS for cross-device pre-send sync. (2) Textarea gets readonly attribute when labStatus is truthy; status text changes to 'Captured in lab sheet' with lock icon. (3) Both surfaces now use 'Lab Notes'. (4) reopenPlanning() now clears labSnapshot=null for clean re-send cycle.

bugs.error_message: Pre-pilot stabilization pass: 5 P1 operational issues — lab notes race condition, misleading dashboard stats, missing confirm on destructive reopen, contradictory state for delivered cases, pipeline counting errors
bugs.root_cause: (A) sendToLab() reads S.labNotes before 800ms debounce fires — snapshot captures stale notes. (B) cDelivered counted labStatus='received' as delivered — inflated stat. (C) reopenPlanning() had no confirm when labStatus truthy — single click destroyed lab workflow state. (D) Lab view 'Reopen Planning' shown for delivered cases. (E) stageFilter.lab used labStatus==='pending' dropping lab-received from all counts.
bugs.fix: (A) _flushPendingNotes() called at top of sendToLab(). (B) cDelivered = p.caseDelivered only; cAtLab = p.labStatus truthy. (C) reopenPlanning() adds context-aware confirm. (D) Reopen Planning hidden when caseDelivered=true. (E) stageFilter.lab uses truthy labStatus; tab renamed 'Lab & Delivery'.
